title: Design decision #1 research: response/file return channel vs the archive's Level B rationale
type: research+design (NON-EDITING: no product source code written)
manager: Opus 4.8 mission manager (RELAUNCHED after predecessor headless turn-end death)
decision_status: NOT MADE. The decision-memo RECOMMENDS; it is INPUT to the ask-chatgpt team lead's decision. Design decision #1 (how assistant responses and patch bundles come back from chatgpt.com) is the team lead's to make and record. This handoff does not record it as decided.

telemetry.ACTUAL: M-001 26m (authoritative wall-clock: start 2026-06-11T22:43:46-05:00 -> end 2026-06-11T23:09:24-05:00; includes the predecessor death + relaunch gap)
telemetry.estimate_vs_actual_note: Estimate was conservative; pi (GPT 5.5 xhigh) workers turned each leg in ~3 min and T1's three lenses ran in parallel.
telemetry.REWORK-CAUSE: spec-gap — the FIRST manager authored worker contracts, dispatched T1a/T1b/T1c, then ended its turn expecting to be 'notified automatically' and its headless `claude -p` process exited. No work was lost (all three T1 reports completed). The charter now carries explicit detached-session discipline (NEVER end turn while children run; hold FOREGROUND blocking watches). Relaunched manager held foreground watches for T2 and T3. Rework cost: ~3 min relaunch gap only.
telemetry.worker_self_timing_unreliable: pi workers self-reported fictional minutes (T2 ACTUAL 44m, T3 ACTUAL 43m) while real per-leg wall-clock was ~3 min each. Worker ESTIMATE/ACTUAL minute self-reports are HALLUCINATED and must not be trusted; derive timing from .pi-workers/<run>/metadata.txt started_at and report END_TIMESTAMP / file mtimes.

what_was_verified.method: Independent verifier T3 (a FRESH pi worker, NOT the T2 synthesizer) re-derived every verdict from ground truth across 5 distinct dimensions; the manager then spot-checked 3 of T3's load-bearing citation claims directly against the archive/README to guard against pi hallucination.

T3_verdicts.CITATIONS: PASS — T3 opened ~40 distinct cited archive locations; all cited files exist and sampled ranges support the adjacent claims.
T3_verdicts.SPEC: PASS — memo claims match README.md:9-26 (signature, session continuity, patch-bundle, loopback-mock acceptance, Level B conflict, library-first).
T3_verdicts.SAFETY: PASS — recommendation designs no network into automated tests (loopback mock only), no credential/cookie/token/profile reads, repo-only writes, archive read-only.
T3_verdicts.COMPLETENESS: PASS — Level B rationale w/ citations; all candidate channels weighed; primary+fallback for BOTH text and bundle; rejected options; mock-fixture section; empirical-unknowns list explicitly covers all 4 required (zip upload limits, real-site downloads, session pinning via URL, model-selection hooks); 120 lines <= 400; decision framed recommendation-only.
T3_verdicts.GROUNDING: PASS — no overclaim; real-site downloads kept operator-gated/unknown; honest evidence-strength labels; memo honestly states no resolved archive claim was found for ToS/ban risk.
T3_verdicts.OVERALL: VERDICT: PASS (verify.md line 94); T3-STATUS: DONE (last line).

what_was_verified.manager_spotcheck: Confirmed 3 of T3's claims against ground truth myself: README.md:9-26 matches the spec claims; DESIGN.md ~888-905 is the genuine Phase-3 ChatUIDriver/UIHealthSignal region; VERIFICATION.md:119-123 literally contains the 'DOM-extraction prohibition + live sentinel tripwire (M-004) | MET' row with dom_artifact_hits=0. T3's verification is grounded in real files, not hallucinated.

artifacts.trust: independently-checked (its citations were re-opened by T3 via the memo; archive-fidelity lens)
producer-only as a standalone report; its conclusions are folded into the verified memo
producer-only as a standalone report; its conclusions are folded into the verified memo
verified-independently (T3 VERDICT: PASS across 5 dimensions + manager spot-check). THIS IS THE PRIMARY DELIVERABLE.
independent verification report; VERDICT: PASS; manager-spot-checked grounded.

memo_recommendation_summary: Recommendation only (team lead decides). Plain text response: PRIMARY copy-button/clipboard (selector-scoped latest turn + completion detector), FALLBACK bounded DOM extraction (latest assistant turn only, no history sweep, adversarial-fixture-tested, explicit completion/truncation markers). Patch-bundle (zip): PRIMARY Playwright file-download capture gated on an operator runbook proving real-site downloads, FALLBACK checksummed fenced base64url zip over the text channel (SHA-256 + manifest + byte count). Connector callback: NOT default; reserved as a later structured/audited mode. The recommendation deliberately departs from Level B's 'never extract assistant output' because the new README makes returned text + patch-bundle retrieval core acceptance obligations; the departure is bounded and operator-gated.
blockers: NONE block this mission — it is complete. The memo's §7 empirical unknowns are OPERATOR-GATED items (deliverables OF the memo, not blockers to it): they can only be resolved by operator-consented runbook runs against the real chatgpt.com on the operator's account.

recommended_next_missions.title: Team lead records design decision #1 from decision-memo.md
recommended_next_missions.note: Read decision-memo.md; decide & record the channel layering (or amend it). Everything below assumes a recorded decision; M-build-text/M-build-bundle can be scoped provisionally to the memo's recommendation if the lead concurs.

recommended_next_missions.title: Build the local mock-ChatGPT fixture per memo §6
recommended_next_missions.note: Loopback-only; per-channel affordances (copy button + clipboard, DOM selector keys w/ stable & virtualized variants, download artifact card serving a real zip with Content-Disposition, fenced-base64 payloads, file-upload input) + adversarial assistant DOM + honest-failure states (login/session-not-found/model-unavailable/upload-unsupported/download-unsupported/truncated/rate-limited). Unblocks zero-network TDD of all 3 use cases. Reuse predecessor phase3_mock_chat.py + phase3_mock_selector_map.json conceptually (READ-ONLY archive).

recommended_next_missions.title: Operator-gated runbook: resolve memo §7 empirical unknowns on real chatgpt.com
recommended_next_missions.note: Observe: zip upload size/type limits; whether/when responses offer file downloads (and Playwright Download event + integrity); session pinning via stored conversation URL/ref across restarts; model-selection UI selectors/persistence. These gate any claim of real-site support for the download-capture bundle path and model selection.

recommended_next_missions.title: Implement ask_chatgpt(...) -> text against the mock
recommended_next_missions.type: implement (single editor, TDD/RED-first; best-of-N design already done by this memo)
recommended_next_missions.note: Copy-button primary + bounded DOM fallback; session_identifier -> conversation_ref/URL mapping; model_settings selection where UI allows; honest failures.

recommended_next_missions.title: Implement patch-bundle round-trip
recommended_next_missions.note: bundle-out (zip + README/catalogue) -> mock GPT edits -> patch-bundle back -> apply locally -> diff matches. Download-capture primary (gated on rec-C for real site) + fenced-base64 fallback with SHA-256/manifest/byte-count validation.

complexity_paradigm_signals: PARADIGM CRUX for the team lead: the recommendation reverses the predecessor's deliberate Level B prohibition on extracting assistant output. The memo argues the product DEFINITION changed (README makes text + patch-bundle retrieval core), and bounds the reader (latest-turn only, no history sweep, adversarial-fixture-tested, operator-gated). The lead must decide whether even a bounded reader is acceptable. This is the heart of design decision #1.

pi worker self-reported ESTIMATE/ACTUAL minutes are HALLUCINATED (off by ~15x). Fix forward: either instruct worker contracts to stamp real `date -Iseconds` at start and end, or derive all worker timing from run-dir metadata (started_at) + report END_TIMESTAMP; do not trust the minute self-reports in telemetry rollups.

SHARED pi-quota ceiling: during this mission another program (programs/pastore-repair: W34/W37/W39) ran 3 concurrent pi workers on the operator's pi account. This mission kept its own footprint at 1 concurrent worker (T2 then T3, sequential). When multiple teams/missions run pi workers concurrently, the operator's pi account is a shared global resource and needs explicit cross-mission allocation (sum of concurrent workers under the account ceiling) — there is no shared limiter.

Headless-manager turn-end death (the predecessor's failure) is now codified in the charter as detached-session discipline. The relaunch recovered with zero lost work because the T1 workers persist their own reports + status files independent of the manager process — the run-dir/status-file pattern is what made recovery cheap.
